title: Beef Stroganoff
cScaleSummary: C1 because the only cholesterol-contributing ingredient is sour cream. The Impossible “Be’f,” vegetables, stock, wine, oil, seasonings, herbs, and cornstarch contribute 0 mg cholesterol. The sour cream contributes a small amount of cholesterol per serving.
ldlActiveSummary: LDL-Active is false because the recipe is low in cholesterol but not actively LDL-supportive. It contains mushrooms and onion, but the structure is not fiber-forward and the sauce relies on sour cream rather than legumes, whole grains, nuts, seeds, or a strongly LDL-supportive base.
intro: Stroganoff comes out of 19th century Russia, built on sautéed beef, sour cream, and a pan sauce enriched by onions and mushrooms. The modern versions lean heavier and muddier. This one keeps the original intent: defined pieces, clean tang, and a sauce that coats without drowning.

stepGroups.title: Sear the Impossible
items: Heat a wide pan over medium-high. Add oil ({{quantity:olive-oil-protein}}).
Add Impossible pieces ({{quantity:impossible-bef-tips}}) in a single layer. Do not crowd.
Let them develop a dark crust before turning.
Cook just until browned, 2 to 3 minutes total.
Remove immediately and set aside.

stepGroups.title: Build the Onion Base
items: Lower heat to medium-low.
Add oil ({{quantity:olive-oil-aromatics}}), onion ({{quantity:yellow-onion}}), and salt ({{quantity:kosher-salt-aromatics}}).
Cook slowly until fully softened and lightly golden, about 12 to 15 minutes.
No browning edges. You are building sweetness.

stepGroups.title: Develop the Mushroom Body
items: Add finely chopped mushrooms first.
Cook until they release moisture and cook down into a paste-like base.
Then add sliced mushrooms and cook until tender and lightly browned.

stepGroups.title: Build Flavor
items: Add garlic ({{quantity:garlic}}), Dijon ({{quantity:dijon-mustard}}), soy sauce ({{quantity:soy-sauce-flavor-build}}), paprika ({{quantity:sweet-paprika}}), and black pepper ({{quantity:black-pepper-flavor-build}}).
Cook 1 minute to bloom.
Add white wine ({{quantity:dry-white-wine}}) and reduce by about half.

stepGroups.title: Form the Sauce
items: Add stock ({{quantity:stock}}) and bring to a simmer.
Cook 5 to 7 minutes to concentrate.

stepGroups.title: Thicken
items: Stir cornstarch ({{quantity:cornstarch}}) with cold water ({{quantity:cold-water}}).
Add to the pan while simmering.
Sauce should lightly coat a spoon, not gel.

stepGroups.title: Finish Gently
items: Lower heat to low.
Temper sour cream ({{quantity:sour-cream}}) with a spoonful of hot sauce, then stir it into the pan.
Do not boil after adding.

stepGroups.title: Return the Impossible
items: Add the seared pieces back in.
Stir just long enough to warm through, about 1 minute.

stepGroups.title: Final Balance
items: Add vinegar ({{quantity:red-wine-vinegar}}), thyme ({{quantity:fresh-thyme}}), and parsley ({{quantity:fresh-parsley}}).
Taste and adjust salt.

stepGroups.title: Serve
items: Serve immediately over noodles, rice, or potatoes.
